# GitKit 🚀

A smart, interactive Git CLI toolkit with an Aircrack-ng inspired terminal UI that simplifies your Git workflow.

## Features

✨ **Smart Push** - Add, commit, and push in one command  
🌿 **Branch Management** - Create, switch, delete, and list branches effortlessly  
🔀 **Intelligent Merging** - Merge branches with conflict detection  
📊 **Enhanced Status** - Beautiful, colorful repository status display  
🎨 **Interactive Mode** - Hacker-style menu-driven interface  
⚙️ **Setup Wizard** - First-run configuration  
📦 **Basic Git Commands** - pull, clone, init, log, diff, fetch  
⚡ **Fast & Lightweight** - Built with performance in mind

## Installation

```bash
npm install -g @vortex77/gitkit
```

## First Run - Setup Wizard

On first use in any project, GitKit will automatically run an interactive setup wizard:

```
╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                                               ║
║     GITKIT CONFIGURATION WIZARD v1.0.0        ║
║                                               ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════╝

  [*] Initializing setup sequence...
  [+] Configuration mode: INTERACTIVE

? [REPO URL] (optional): https://github.com/user/repo
? [DEFAULT BRANCH]: main
? [AUTO-PUSH] after commit? Yes
? [AUTO-FETCH] before operations? No
? [COMMIT STYLE]: Simple (free-form)
```

This creates a `.gitkit.json` file in your project with your preferences. You can reconfigure anytime with:

```bash
gitkit setup
```

## Usage

### Interactive Mode

Simply run `gitkit` without any arguments to enter interactive mode:

```bash
gitkit
```

You'll see the Aircrack-ng style interface:

```
  ██████╗ ██╗████████╗██╗  ██╗██╗████████╗
 ██╔════╝ ██║╚══██╔══╝██║ ██╔╝██║╚══██╔══╝
 ██║  ███╗██║   ██║   █████╔╝ ██║   ██║   
 ██║   ██║██║   ██║   ██╔═██╗ ██║   ██║   
 ╚██████╔╝██║   ██║   ██║  ██╗██║   ██║   
  ╚═════╝ ╚═╝   ╚═╝   ╚═╝  ╚═╝╚═╝   ╚═╝   

  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
  │  Advanced Git Toolkit v1.0.0               │
  │  [+] System loaded                         │
  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════

  ═══════════════════════════════════════════════
? [SELECT OPERATION]
  [1] Smart Push → add, commit, push
  [2] Branch Management → create, switch, delete
  [3] Merge Branch → merge operations
  [4] Repository Status → view changes
  [X] Exit
```

### Command-Line Mode

#### Setup

```bash
# Run setup wizard
gitkit setup
```

#### Smart Push

Add all changes, commit, and push in one command:

```bash
gitkit push "your commit message"
```

#### Basic Git Commands

```bash
# Pull changes
gitkit pull
gitkit pull -r origin -b develop

# Clone a repository
gitkit clone https://github.com/user/repo
gitkit clone https://github.com/user/repo my-folder

# Initialize a new repository
gitkit init

# View commit history
gitkit log
gitkit log -n 20  # show last 20 commits

# Show changes
gitkit diff
gitkit diff package.json  # diff specific file

# Fetch from remote
gitkit fetch
gitkit fetch -r upstream
```

#### Branch Management

```bash
# List all branches
gitkit branch list
gitkit branch ls

# Create a new branch
gitkit branch create feature/new-feature

# Switch to a branch
gitkit branch switch main
gitkit branch sw main

# Delete a branch
gitkit branch delete old-branch
gitkit branch del old-branch -f  # force delete
```

#### Merge

```bash
# Merge a branch into current branch
gitkit merge feature/my-feature
```

#### Status

```bash
# View repository status
gitkit status
gitkit st
```

#### Help

```bash
# View all commands
gitkit --help

# View help for specific command
gitkit push --help
gitkit branch --help
```

## Configuration

GitKit stores project-specific configuration in `.gitkit.json`:

```json
{
  "repoUrl": "https://github.com/user/repo",
  "defaultBranch": "main",
  "autoPush": true,
  "autoFetch": false,
  "commitStyle": "simple",
  "customPrefix": null,
  "setupDate": "2025-12-08T14:30:00.000Z"
}
```

You can:
- Commit this file to share settings with your team
- Add to `.gitignore` to keep settings local
- Reconfigure anytime with `gitkit setup`

## Examples

### Typical Workflow

```bash
# First run - setup wizard appears
gitkit

# Check status
gitkit status

# Create a new feature branch
gitkit branch create feature/awesome-feature

# Make your changes...

# Smart push
gitkit push "Add awesome feature"

# Switch back to main
gitkit branch switch main

# Merge your feature
gitkit merge feature/awesome-feature

# Delete the feature branch
gitkit branch delete feature/awesome-feature
```

### Interactive Workflow

```bash
# Start interactive mode
gitkit

# Follow the prompts:
# 1. Select "Branch Management"
# 2. Select "Create new branch"
# 3. Enter branch name
# 4. Make changes
# 5. Select "Smart Push"
# 6. Enter commit message
```

## UI Style

GitKit features an **Aircrack-ng inspired interface** with:
- ASCII art banner
- Technical bracket notation `[*]`, `[+]`, `[!]`
- Green/gray/white color scheme
- Visual separators for clear sections
- Monospace technical formatting

## Requirements

- Node.js 14 or higher
- Git installed and configured
- A Git repository (run from within a Git repo)

## Technologies

Built with modern Node.js libraries:

- **Commander.js** - Command-line interface framework
- **Inquirer.js** - Interactive command-line prompts
- **Chalk** - Terminal string styling
- **Ora** - Elegant terminal spinners
- **simple-git** - Git command wrapper
